expectations.name: skill_invoked
expectations.evidence: No skill invocation — this is the without_skill baseline.

expectations.name: modularity_mapped
expectations.text: Modularity mapped to a dependency check tool (dependency-cruiser or similar)
expectations.evidence: Mapped to madge --circular (JS/TS), ArchUnit (Java), import-linter (Python). Multiple tool options per stack provided.

expectations.name: coverage_mapped
expectations.text: Testability mapped to a coverage tool (jest --coverage or similar)
expectations.evidence: Mapped to jest --coverage (JS), JaCoCo (Java), pytest-cov (Python). Coverage threshold 80% correctly used.

expectations.name: performance_mapped
expectations.text: Performance mapped to a load testing tool (autocannon, k6 or similar)
expectations.evidence: Mapped to k6, Gatling, JMeter, Artillery. Also includes synthetic monitoring and APM suggestions.

expectations.name: concrete_commands
expectations.text: Specific run commands provided for each fitness function (not just tool names)
expectations.evidence: Tool names and high-level config snippets provided, but no concrete run commands like 'npx depcruise src'. The output is more of a strategy document than actionable commands. Commands are embedded in CI YAML examples but not as standalone run instructions.

expectations.name: thresholds_from_arch
expectations.text: Thresholds come from architecture.md (80% coverage, <500ms) not invented
expectations.evidence: Coverage 80% and performance p95 <500ms match architecture.md. No invented thresholds.
